name: Azure SDK for Python
what_it_does: Official Azure SDK for Python. Modular packages per Azure service: azure-storage-blob, azure-cosmos, azure-servicebus, azure-ai-openai, azure-identity, azure-keyvault-secrets, and 200+ others. Follows Azure SDK design guidelines with consistent patterns across all services — same credential types, same retry policies, same paging patterns. Supports both sync and async (asyncio) variants for each service client.
use_cases: Store and retrieve files from Azure Blob Storage for agent data pipelines and artifact management using azure-storage-blob
Use Azure OpenAI Service (GPT-4, DALL-E) from Python agents via azure-ai-openai with the same API as OpenAI SDK
Read/write Azure Cosmos DB documents for globally distributed agent state with azure-cosmos multi-region support
Send/receive messages via Azure Service Bus for async agent communication using azure-servicebus
Retrieve secrets from Azure Key Vault for secure agent credential management using azure-keyvault-secrets
not_for: AWS services — use boto3 for AWS; Azure SDK is Azure-specific
GCP services — use Google Cloud Python libraries for GCP
Multi-cloud abstraction — use cloud-agnostic libraries for provider independence
best_when: You're building Python applications in Azure-centric environments (especially with Azure OpenAI, Cosmos DB, or when organization mandates Azure) with consistent SDK patterns across services.
avoid_when: You're on AWS or GCP, or need a cloud-agnostic SDK.

auth.notes: azure-identity provides DefaultAzureCredential which tries: env vars, Managed Identity, Azure CLI, Visual Studio, etc. Managed Identity is best practice for production Azure workloads. Service Principal with client secret or certificate for CI/CD. API keys for simpler services (Cognitive Services, OpenAI).

pricing.free_tier_limits: SDK is free; Azure services have free tiers and $200 credit for new accounts
pricing.paid_tiers: Azure service costs apply per service
pricing.notes: MIT licensed client libraries are free. Azure service costs apply based on usage. Azure free tier covers many services with monthly limits.

performance.rate_limits: Per-service limits; Azure OpenAI has TPM/RPM limits per deployment

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: azure.core.exceptions provides HttpResponseError with status_code and error.code. Each service has typed exception subclasses. ResourceNotFoundError, ClientAuthenticationError are common typed exceptions. Error messages include correlation IDs for support.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. Blob upload overwrites idempotently. Cosmos DB upsert is idempotent. Service Bus message sends are not idempotent — can duplicate.
known_agent_gotchas: DefaultAzureCredential tries multiple auth methods in order — locally it may use Azure CLI creds that differ from production Managed Identity; always test with production-equivalent credentials
Azure OpenAI endpoint and API version must both be specified — different from OpenAI SDK; endpoint is your Azure resource URL, not api.openai.com
Async clients require explicit .close() or async context manager — resource leaks from unclosed clients are common; always use 'async with' pattern
Cosmos DB partition key must be specified for all operations — missing partition key causes 400 errors; always include partitionKey in queries and item operations
Rate limiting differs by service and SKU — Azure OpenAI TPM limits per deployment, Storage request limits per account; monitor with Azure Monitor metrics
Service Bus message lock renewal needed for long-processing messages — lock expires after lockDuration; use auto-lock-renewal or manual renewal to prevent message reappearing

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Managed Identity eliminates credential management. Azure Key Vault integration for secrets. RBAC with Azure AD roles. Strong compliance portfolio (FedRAMP, HIPAA, SOC2).
